sections.header: Verses 1–9 — “Is Not This the Kind of Fasting I Have Chosen?”

heb.paragraph: "Is not this the kind of fasting (ṣōm) I have chosen: to loose the chains of injustice and untie the cords of the yoke, to set the oppressed free and break every yoke?" (v.6). The people fast and complain that God does not notice (v.3). God answer: your fasting ends in quarrels and strikes (v.4). True fasting is not sackcloth and ashes but justice: loose chains, free the oppressed, share food with the hungry, shelter the homeless, clothe the naked (vv.6–7). "Then your light will break forth like the dawn, and your healing will quickly appear" (v.8). "Then you will call, and the LORD will answer" (v.9). The equation: justice produces answered prayer.

notes.note: "Why have we fasted and you have not seen it? Yet on the day of your fasting you do as you please and exploit your workers." Fasting that coexists with exploitation is rejected. The ritual without the ethic is void.

"Is not this the kind of fasting I have chosen: loose chains, free the oppressed, share food, shelter the homeless, clothe the naked?" Six actions. Not religious acts but justice acts. God chosen fast is social action.
"Then your light will break forth like the dawn. Then you will call, and the LORD will answer." The conditional: do justice THEN light comes, THEN prayer is answered. The sequence is non-negotiable.

sections.header: Verses 10–14 — “You Will Be Called Repairer of Broken Walls”

heb.paragraph: "You will be called Repairer of Broken Walls (gādēr), Restorer of Streets with Dwellings" (v.12). "If you spend yourselves on behalf of the hungry and satisfy the needs of the oppressed, then your light will rise in the darkness, and your night will become like the noonday" (v.10). "The LORD will guide you always; he will satisfy your needs in a sun-scorched land and will strengthen your frame. You will be like a well-watered garden, like a spring whose waters never fail" (v.11). The titles: Repairer, Restorer. The reward: well-watered garden, unfailing spring. The Sabbath is honoured not as legalism but as delight (vv.13–14).

notes.note: "Your light will rise in the darkness. You will be like a well-watered garden, a spring whose waters never fail." The garden metaphor: justice-doers become Edens. Their lives irrigate others.
"Repairer of Broken Walls, Restorer of Streets with Dwellings." The identity earned by justice: not destroyer but repairer. The names are vocational, not honorary.

ppl.name: LORD
ppl.text: Then you will call, and the LORD will answer…

chapter_panels.trans: VersionTranslationNIVFor day after day they seek me out; they seem eager to know my ways, as if they were a nation that does what is right and has not forsaken the commands of its God. They ask me for just decisions and seem eager for God to come near them.ESVYet they seek me daily and delight to know my ways, as if they were a nation that did righteousness and did not forsake the judgment of their God; they ask of me righteous judgments; they delight to draw near to God.NIVYour fasting ends in quarreling and strife, and in striking each other with wicked fists. You cannot fast as you do today and expect your voice to be heard on high.ESVBehold, you fast only to quarrel and to fight and to hit with a wicked fist. Fasting like yours this day will not make your voice to be heard on high.

rows.label: Repairer of Walls — 58:10–14
rows.text: "REPAIRER of broken walls, RESTORER of streets." Well-watered garden. "Your light will rise in the DARKNESS."

hebtext.note: "Is not this the kind of fasting (ṣōm) I have chosen: to loose the chains of injustice and untie the cords of the yoke, to set the oppressed free and break every yoke?" (v.6). The people fast and complain that God does not notice (v.3). God answer: your fasting ends in quarrels and strikes (v.4). True fasting is not sackcloth and ashes but justice: loose chains, free the oppressed, share food with the hungry, shelter the homeless, clothe the naked (vv.6–7). "Then your light will break forth like the dawn, and your healing will quickly appear" (v.8). "Then you will call, and the LORD will answer" (v.9). The equation: justice produces answered prayer.

"You will be called Repairer of Broken Walls (gādēr), Restorer of Streets with Dwellings" (v.12). "If you spend yourselves on behalf of the hungry and satisfy the needs of the oppressed, then your light will rise in the darkness, and your night will become like the noonday" (v.10). "The LORD will guide you always; he will satisfy your needs in a sun-scorched land and will strengthen your frame. You will be like a well-watered garden, like a spring whose waters never fail" (v.11). The titles: Repairer, Restorer. The reward: well-watered garden, unfailing spring. The Sabbath is honoured not as legalism but as delight (vv.13–14).

themes.note: Justice peaks at 10: true fasting = social action. Loose chains, feed hungry, clothe naked. Justice produces answered prayer and light. Repairer of walls.
